Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: White House is pushing legislation that would cut the number of legal immigrants allowed

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: into the United States in half and reshape how people come to live in this country.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: The bill, introduced by Republican senators Tom Cotton and David Perdue, would replace

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: the current lottery system for issuing green cards with a merit-based system, favoring

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: those who speak English and who have higher-level job skills.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: The number of green cards issued every year would go from just over a million to about 500,000.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: press briefing and when it was time for questions, things got heated.
